essence: At Chiang Mai Coffee Club, Yash (ex-corporate-lawyer turned automation builder) presents AI automation for small teams — smart outreach at 3000-5000 personalized messages, 24/7 knowledge-trained assistants, and AI voice receptionists — arguing future winners have systems that run while they rest.

David leads a Claude Lab live session in Chiang Mai covering his AI video toolchain, an ElevenLabs voice agent wired into FliHub, the Ralph Wiggum loop (1,524 auto-generated unit tests in 1.5 days), his personal agentic OS vision, BMAD agent roles, and secrets/sandboxing mitigations for Claude Code.

David dictates the design brief for the Tech Stack Hotel — an adaptable JSON schema with strict schema/data/HTML separation, an 8-bit game-style hotel visualization of his agentic OS tech stack, OpenClaw-inspired assistant architecture docs per room, and a visual index of all agentic-OS second-brain files.

actions.text: Create the Tech Stack Hotel JSON schema first, with self-updating rules so JSON documents follow schema changes
Build an HTML shell view (game-like 8-bit hotel) that reads external JSON data for floors/rooms
Create a visual index of all Agentic OS documentation files in the second brain
Write design guidelines keeping layout separate from visual aesthetics for HTML/game graphics generation

essence: David refines the Tech Stack Hotel model — infrastructure/networking (Tailscale) belongs as substrate below the building not at the top, and every tool integrated into the agent OS should also get its own standalone second brain the OS merely references.

actions.text: Create a Tailscale-focused standalone second brain referenced by the agent OS

essence: After losing two closed AI conversations, David asks what the second brain already holds on SSH/remote computer control and whether a companion remote-access application had been under consideration.

Via voice agent David lists FliHub projects matching 'opus' (B72, 793, P75, P765, B89, C14 etc.), pulls the B72 transcript, and delegates git add/commit/push across the 4-5 projects whose package dependencies were updated, assuming minimal testing means they work.

actions.text: Git add, commit, and push the 4-5 Opus video projects with updated package dependencies

essence: Recovering from 24 hours of illness, David preps tomorrow's team direction call — 10-15 minutes with the team, assistant to act as his memory/context loader with a 2-3 minute status summary — plus housekeeping to delete outdated installation and prompt files.

actions.text: Delete the outdated installation file and prompt file, then move the remaining files
Prepare a 2-3 minute project status summary to context-load David at tomorrow's team call

essence: Fragmentary debugging note — Postgres lazy-loading of RBM for licensing/commands isn't working and SQL profiling is needed on a bugged-out state in an unnamed project; no concrete next steps captured.

David reviews Steve's (Dreaming Computers, Chiang Mai) first ComfyUI video on kie.ai API nodes — grid batching, face consistency, Kling 3 credit economics, preflight checks — forwards it to a team member as a learning resource and records feedback that Steve should ship the ComfyUI JSON plus a blog post into his Skool community as a signup incentive.

actions.text: Team member to watch, understand, and potentially reproduce Steve's ComfyUI kie.ai workflow
Send Steve the recorded feedback: publish the ComfyUI JSON + auto-generated blog post in his Skool community
